issue_title: conda index on object storage
issue_body:
Hi! I'm currently investigating conda at Counsyl.  I've been looking through the docs on `conda index` and setting up an internal index for compiled binaries (we already have an internal pypi server).

Looking through the docs and implementation, seems like you could host a conda channel directly on object storage without too much work; the only issue is generating the repodata.json files.

Is there a way to tell conda index to crawl a repository over http but dump output files somewhere else?  Would that be an easy use case to support?  (then setting up a custom conda channel becomes as easy as syncing the output of build directories from running conda-bld, then dumping the results on to s3).

Failing that, is there a standard web server folks suggest to use for private channels? (It doesn't have to support authentication - VPN-only access is sufficient).

issue_title: Can not activate/deactivate conda environment due to cmder lambda character not handled in conda encoder/decoder
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior I have updated to conda from version=4.3.29 to version=4.5.4, and I can not activate by conda environment after the update. I'm using cmder bash terminal on windows 10. When executing the command: `source activate py36_all`, it outputs ``` # >>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> ERROR REPORT <<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<< Traceback (most recent call last): File "C:\Users\FOGH\AppData\Local\Continuum\anaconda3\lib\site-packages\conda\cli\main.py", line 98, in main return activator_main() File "C:\Users\FOGH\AppData\Local\Continuum\anaconda3\lib\site-packages\conda\activate.py", line 584, in main print(activator.execute(), end='') File "C:\Users\FOGH\AppData\Local\Continuum\anaconda3\lib\encodings\cp1252.py", line 19, in encode return codecs.charmap_encode(input,self.errors,encoding_table)[0] UnicodeEncodeError: 'charmap' codec can't encode character '\u03bb' in position 130: character maps to `$ C:\Users\FOGH\AppData\Local\Continuum\anaconda3\Scripts\conda shell.posix activate py36_all` ... Followed by the whole environment variables error print out and report submission... ``` Note that cmder have a lambda charater in front of each command. I think it is this lambda charater (i.e. '\u03bb') that is not handled correctly in the encoder/decoder class found in \lib\encodings\cp1252.py. I have tried to add add the string '\u03bb' to the `decoding_table` tuple list at line 47 in \lib\encodings\cp1252.py, and it results in that the environment can be loaded, but then deactivating the environment with `source deactivate` the error occurs again, with the following error: ``` # >>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>> ERROR REPORT <<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<< Traceback (most recent call last): File "C:\Users\FOGH\AppData\Local\Continuum\anaconda3\lib\site-packages\conda\cli\main.py", line 98, in main return activator_main() File "C:\Users\FOGH\AppData\Local\Continuum\anaconda3\lib\site-packages\conda\activate.py", line 584, in main print(activator.execute(), end='') File "C:\Users\FOGH\AppData\Local\Continuum\anaconda3\lib\encodings\cp1252.py", line 19, in encode return codecs.charmap_encode(input,self.errors,encoding_table)[0] UnicodeEncodeError: 'charmap' codec can't encode character '\x8d' in position 349: character maps to ... Again followed by the whole environment variables error print out and report submission... ``` where '\x8d' is the encode value of the next element in `decoding_table` after where i have added '\u03bb'. i.e. ``` '\u03bb' # '\ufffe' # 0x8D -> UNDEFINED ``` Do any one know how to add the lambda charater to the encoder/decoder class, such that i can activate and deactivate the conda environment in my cmder bash terminal? ## Expected Behavior Well, I should be able to activate and deactivate the conda environment. ## Environment Information

issue_title: Conda overriding or circumventing ACL permissions in linux.
issue_body:
This references #1329 on conda for group installations.

We've been doing some experimentation with using ACLs on a group conda installation and they are not working. Somehow, when conda installs things, it is able to apply permissions that are overriding or circumventing the ACLs.

We created a sandbox directory `permstest`. 
```
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ getfacl .
# file: .
# owner: burchard
# group: BioCoders
# flags: -st
user::rwx
group::rwx
other::---
default:user::rwx
default:group::rwx
default:group:BioCoders:rwx
default:mask::rwx
default:other::---
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ getfacl miniconda3
# file: miniconda3
# owner: balter
# group: BioCoders
# flags: -s-
user::rwx
group::rwx
group:BioCoders:rwx
mask::rwx
other::---
default:user::rwx
default:group::rwx
default:group:BioCoders:rwx
default:mask::rwx
default:other::---
```
When I or anyone else creates files or directories in that directory, everything "works":

```
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ echo "main (){}" > test.c
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ gcc test.c
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ touch touchtest
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ mkdir dirtest
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ ./miniconda3/bin/python -c "f=open('filecreatedbypy','w'); f.write('test'); f.close()"
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ ./miniconda3/bin/python -c "import os; os.mkdir('dirbypy')"
balter@exalab3:.../permstest$ ls -lrt
total 19875
-rw-rw----+  1 balter   BioCoders 33905474 Feb  2 23:05 Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh
drwxrws---+ 12 balter   BioCoders     4096 Feb  3 09:05 miniconda3
-rw-rw----+  1 balter   BioCoders       10 Feb  3 12:07 test.c
-rwxrwx--x+  1 balter   BioCoders     6262 Feb  3 12:08 a.out
-rw-rw----+  1 balter   BioCoders        0 Feb  3 12:09 touchtest
drwxrws---+  2 balter   BioCoders     4096 Feb  3 12:09 dirtest
-rw-rw----+  1 balter   BioCoders        4 Feb  3 12:09 filecreatedbypy
drwxrws---+  2 balter   BioCoders     4096 Feb  3 12:09 dirbypy
```

When I installed miniconda, the permissions went in as 770 as hoped for. However, when I or anyone else installs, they are going in as 755. This means that only the installer will be able to remove, update, etc.

Conda is simply a python program, and it invokes other compilers and installers. So based on the experiments above, it seems like anything done with a python program or system compiler should result in the expected permissions.

But, clearly conda is able to do something we don't understand regarding how it is able to override permissions.

Any clarity that can be shed on this would be greatly appreciated!

issue_title: conda update --all creates update loop
issue_body:
Starting from a fresh install of anaconda 4.3.1 using python 2.7,  `conda update --all` results in an upgrade of a list of packages, coupled with an upgrade to anaconda custom-py27_0
After this update is run `conda update --all` then suggests an update of anaconda custom-py27_0 back to 4.3.1-np111py27_0, along with a downgrade of all the upgraded packages. This loop appears to continue if the downgrade is run.

issue_title: conda-build does not run install_headers command when package uses setuptools
issue_body:
Distutils' command `distutils.command.install_headers` installs C/C++ header files into the Python include directory. The list of these header files is specified with the `headers` keyword argument in `distutils.core.setup`.

However, in the case when the package's setup.py uses setuptools instead of distutils, the headers don't get installed when simply running `python setup.py install`. Still, `pip install` does install the headers.

Since conda-build runs `python setup.py install`, it does not detect the header installation.

The reason why pip detects the headers seems to be that pip runs `python setup.py install` with a flag, that is `--single-version-externally-managed`. Should conda-build use the same flag as pip?

issue_title: Index files provided to VariantFile not always used
issue_body:
When opening a remove variant file using the `index_filename`` parameter, this index is ignored if a remote index exists. For example, if we download the index below to /tmp and run this code (not from /tmp)
```python
import pysam

vf = pysam.VariantFile(
        "http://ftp.1000genomes.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/ftp/phase3/integrated_sv_map/ALL.autosomes.pindel.20130502.complexindex.low_coverage.genotypes.vcf.gz",
        index_filename="/tmp/ALL.autosomes.pindel.20130502.complexindex.low_coverage.genotypes.vcf.gz.tbi")
```
we get 
```
[W::bcf_hdr_check_sanity] PL should be declared as Number=G
[M::test_and_fetch] downloading file 'http://ftp.1000genomes.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/ftp/phase3/integrated_sv_map/ALL.autosomes.pindel.20130502.complexindex.low_coverage.genotypes.vcf.gz.tbi' to local directory
```
and the index file is left in the current working directory.

See also https://github.com/ga4gh/server/issues/1210.

issue_title: How to use VUnit in a larger framework through a lower-level interface
issue_body:
The [PoC-Library](https://github.com/VLSI-EDA/PoC) contains her own:
- source file dependency management
- simulator abstraction layer
- testbench naming scheme

so no high-level `run.py` is needed. Using the user API intended for `run.py` would double the work. I'm looking for low-level APIs to get access to VUnits features like:
- VHDL runner
  - running single test cases
  - running test cases in parallel
  - running test cases in sequence
  - restart failing test cases
- jUnit report files
  - these files shall be readable by GitLab ([planned for 10.5 - 22.02.2018](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-ce/issues/17081))

My initial question might be to broad, so please direct me to create a good issue also for other users as a reference.

**Main questions:**
- How to create the `runner_cfg` generic for VHDL test benches?
  - What information are encoded in that generic?
- How does a VUnit testbench communicate back to the Python scripts?

I found important code at lines [194-214 in test_suites.py](https://github.com/VUnit/vunit/blob/master/vunit/test_suites.py?ts=2#L194-L214).

issue_title: Set `application/x-ndjson` content type on bulk requests

cc @cnelson @wjwoodson

issue_title: az ad sp create-for-rbac: flakiness
issue_body:
The following cmd sometimes returns error, but the actual AAD app is created (but not the SP):

```
$ az ad sp create-for-rbac --scopes /subscriptions/xxxxxx/resourcegroups/gggggg/providers/Microsoft.ContainerRegistry/registries/nnnn \
 --role Owner --password "$azure_client_secret" \
  --years 2 --name http://foo-bar-ahmetb/

ERROR: Resource 'ServicePrincipal_00e1c5b1-394e-48ca-a1b7-f39b77635928' does not exist or one of its queried reference-property objects are not present.
```

When I do `az ad sp show --id http://foo-bar-ahmetb/`, I got error:

    ERROR: service principal 'http://foo-bar-ahmetb/' doesn't exist

but `az ad app show --id http://foo-bar-ahmetb/` worked just fine. 

So it looks like `create-for-rbac` probably is running into some replication delay issue or somethign else from AAD and therefore erroring out, possibly having created the AAD App but not the SP.

issue_title: Simply the AZ login experience
issue_body:
The current experience for signing into Azure from the command line feels sluggish and requires multiple steps - here are some suggestions for simplifying this experience:

1.)  When you enter the command "az login" it takes several seconds without any indication that something is happening - at first, I wasn't sure it was working and I cancelled out of the command. To help with this, the command should provide some sort of output to the console to indicate that it's doing something.

2.) After entering the "az login" command, it eventually says "To sign in, use a web browser to open the page https://aka.ms/devicelogin and enter the code XXXXX to authenticate".  After the user enters this code into the browser, then they can log-in with their azure creds. This part of the experience could be improved by either allowing the creds to be entered directly in the command line without having the user go to the browser. Or, at least the browser could be automatically launched and the code already entered for them so that they don't have to manually launch the browser and enter in the code.

issue_title: Swallows successive multiple slashes in blob URLs
issue_body:
Blob storage supports blob names like: `http://.../container/blob//name.txt` (in this case blob name should be `blob//name.txt` (if you try to fetch `blob/name.txt` via HTTP client, it won't work).

Storage account swallows successive multiple slashes when 

1. creating the blobs
2. getting the blob [...properties etc]

When I try to create a blob like:

    bs.put_blob('foo','has//double//slashes','','BlockBlob')

Its name shows up as `has/double/slashes` in the backend and when I try to fetch it:

    bs.get_blob_properties('foo', 'has//double//slashes')
    bs.get_blob_properties('foo', 'has/double/slashes')

they both return success. This should not be true as `has/double/slashes` and `has//double//slashes` are different resources according to Azure Blob Storage (but not to the URL specification –that's by design in Azure Blob Storage; but IMO, a mistaken one).

In any case, this is a bug.

issue_title: Make stats polling asynchronous
issue_body:
Greetings all!

I'm looking to transition a project I curate (omniduct, a library to simplify data acquisition, especially for data scientists) from `pyhive` to `prestodb`; but currently I would lose the ability to poll for query progress before actually attempting to retrieve results.

i.e. `cursor.fetchone()` is used to both collect results and update stats, which means that I cannot show progress of the actual execution of the query, only progress through collection of the results.

Would you welcome a patch to add support for this polling? Or are you planning to add it yourselves? Or are you opposed to adding this feature?

issue_title: pre-commit bug when invoking eslint via npm
issue_body:
Bug reproduction:

```shell
mkdir tmp && cd tmp
git init && pre-commit install
cat <<EOF >.pre-commit-config.yaml
-   repo: local
    hooks:
    -   id: validate-js
        name: validate-js
        language: system
        entry: sh -c 'npm run validate-js'
        files: .js$
EOF
cat <<EOF >package.json
{
  "devDependencies": {
    "eslint": "2.2.0"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "validate-js": "eslint broken.js"
  }
}
EOF
echo 'foo {' >broken.js
git add .pre-commit-config.yaml package.json broken.js
npm install && pre-commit run
```

The execution then hangs.
There is the stacktrace following a `CTRL+C` :
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/lucas_cimon/.local/share/virtualenvs/pre-commit-bug/bin/pre-commit", line 11, in <module>

    return _run_hooks(repo_hooks, args, write, environ)
  File "/home/lucas_cimon/.local/share/virtualenvs/pre-commit-bug/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pre_commit/commands/run.py", line 136, in _run_hooks
    retval |= _run_single_hook(hook, repo, args, write, skips)
  File "/home/lucas_cimon/.local/share/virtualenvs/pre-commit-bug/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pre_commit/commands/run.py", line 89, in _run_single_hook
    retcode, stdout, stderr = repo.run_hook(hook, tuple(filenames))
  File "/home/lucas_cimon/.local/share/virtualenvs/pre-commit-bug/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pre_commit/repository.py", line 199, in run_hook
    self.cmd_runner, hook, file_args,
  File "/home/lucas_cimon/.local/share/virtualenvs/pre-commit-bug/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pre_commit/languages/system.py", line 23, in run_hook
    tuple(shlex.split(hook['entry'])) + tuple(hook['args']), file_args,
  File "/home/lucas_cimon/.local/share/virtualenvs/pre-commit-bug/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pre_commit/xargs.py", line 58, in xargs
    *run_cmd, encoding=None, retcode=None
  File "/home/lucas_cimon/.local/share/virtualenvs/pre-commit-bug/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pre_commit/util.py", line 194, in cmd_output
    stdout, stderr = proc.communicate()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/subprocess.py", line 960, in communicate
    stdout, stderr = self._communicate(input, endtime, timeout)

KeyboardInterrupt
```

I'm using `pre-commit` version `0.8.2`.

I found out a fix: adding `shell=True` to `popen_kwargs` in [`util.py:cmd_output`](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit/blob/master/pre_commit/util.py#L190).

Now I can submit a pull request, but the question is :
- should we let the user configure "manually" a `system` hook with `shell=True` in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (this is my preference, but this is a quite obscure setting option we should document clearly)

- should we add a new type of "shell" hook, very similar to "system" with only this little difference (IMHO: probably overkill and a lotta more code to add)

What do you think ?

issue_title: Unexplained behaviour when training GaussianHMM with 12 states
issue_body:
Hi,

I've come across a weird behaviour that I can't explain. I'm quite new to both Python, HmmLearn and HMMs. I wonder whether you could help me out and explain the behaviour to me.

Basically, I create `n` observed groups of Gaussian emissions with means that are 100.0 apart and standard deviations of 1.0. This implies that there's almost no likelihood of one group's Gaussian distribution overlapping another group's Gaussian distribution. I then create a sequence of emissions such that each emission belonging to one group precedes and proceeds only emissions belonging to one other group.

Here's a sample of the groups and the emissions:

```
Group   	      0	      1	      2	      0	      1	      2	      0	      1	      2	      0	      1	      2
Emission	    0.9	   98.6	  199.3	    0.4	   99.0	  199.9	    0.2	   99.2	  198.7	    0.2	  101.0	  199.4
```

When I fit a HMM with `n` hidden states, I expect that each hidden state would have a one-to-one relationship with a group of emissions and hence each state would have a transition probability of exactly 1.0 with only one other state.

Here's a sample of the transition matrix for `n=3`:

```
       	  199.3	    0.4	   99.4
  199.3	0.00000	1.00000	0.00000
    0.4	0.00000	0.00000	1.00000
   99.4	1.00000	0.00000	0.00000
```

This works well for values of `n` between 1 and 11 (inclusive), but seems to suddenly break-down when `n=12`. I've tried decreasing the convergence threshold, increasing the number of iterations and increasing the length of the sequence to fit the HMM with. None of this worked. It's as if there's something magical about the number 12. Is there?

I've given my code below. I'm using python 3.5.2, hmmlearn v0.2.0, numpy v1.11.2 and scikit-learn v0.18.2.

```
import hmmlearn.hmm as hmm
import random

random.seed(0)

# number if different groups of emissions to observe and their characteristics
group_count = 12
group_mean = [i * 100 for i in range(0, group_count)]
group_sd = [1.0 for i in range(0, group_count)]

# number of different hidden states to use to represent the pattern
#   fixed to be equal to the number of groups
hidden_state_count = group_count

# create the sequence of emissions, one from each group such that
#   each group always precedes and proceeds one other group
# replicate this pattern several times
sequence_groups = [i for i in range(0, group_count)] * 4

# create the observations from the things to observe
sequence_emissions = [random.gauss(group_mean[i], group_sd[i])
                      for i in sequence_groups]

print('Group   ', *[("%7d" % i) for i in sequence_groups], sep='\t')
print('Emission', *[("%7.1f" % i) for i in sequence_emissions], sep='\t')

# create the HMM object
h = hmm.GaussianHMM(n_components=hidden_state_count,
                    random_state=1,
                    tol=0.000001,
                    n_iter=10000)

# convert the observations to the required format to fit with
training_data = [[i] for i in sequence_emissions]

# fit the HMM using the training data
h.fit(training_data)

# print out the transition matrix
print('%7s' % '',
      *["%7.1f" % x for x in h.means_], sep='\t')
for i, l in enumerate(h.transmat_):
    print("%7.1f" % h.means_[i],
          *["%7.5f" % x for x in l.tolist()], sep='\t')

```

Here are the emissions and transition matrix for `n=12`:

issue_title: Exception handling doesn't close connection
issue_body:
## Long story short

With certain malformed urls, the connection is not properly closed.   This causes an "Unclosed connection".

## Expected behaviour

Connection cleaned up when there is an exception writing headers.

## Actual behaviour

Connection not properly cleaned up.

## Steps to reproduce
Here are two examples, one mocked and one using a live site.  Both should produce an "Unclosed connection" error.

requires pytest and [aresponses](https://github.com/circleup/aresponses)
```python
@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_invalid_location(aresponses):
    # real example: 'http://www.skinhealthcanada.com/index.cfm?pagepath=Brands&id=66172'
    url = 'http://badwebsite.circleup.com/'
    bad_url = 'http://badwebsite.circleup.com/in\udcaedex.html'

    aresponses.add('badwebsite.circleup.com', aresponses.ANY, 'GET', aresponses.Response(status=302, headers={'Location': bad_url}))
    aresponses.add('badwebsite.circleup.com', aresponses.ANY, 'GET', aresponses.Response())

    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        try:

                    try:

issue_title: Parser of the Forwarded header ignores the port number
issue_body:
## Long story short

Parser of the `Forwarded` header ignores the port number.

## Expected behaviour

Parsing the header `Forwarded: for=1.2.3.4:5678` should yield a `request.forwarded` value of `({"for": "1.2.3.4:5678"},)`.

## Actual behaviour

Parsing the header `Forwarded: for=1.2.3.4:5678` gives me `request.forwarded` value of `({"for": "1.2.3.4"},)`.  That is, it removes the client port number.

## Your environment

aiohttp 3.2.1 / Python 3.6.5.

issue_title: Encoding in POST data
issue_body:
## Long story short

I'm doing a `POST` request via `client.post`:

```
data = aiohttp.FormData({
            'FindText': name,
        }, charset='windows-1251')

client.post(base_url, params={'RowFrom': offset}, data=data)
```

POST data should contain: `FindText=%D5%E0%ED`

## Actual behaviour

`FindText=%D1%85%D0%B0%D0%BD'`

Looking through the code of `formdata.py:99`

```
urlencode(data, doseq=True).encode(charset),
```

I noticed, that `data` is urlencoded in UTF-8 first and then encoded to `windows-1251` (and that has no effect on `%D1...`).

For now, I just manually do in my code:

data = urlencode({
            'FindText': name,
        }, encoding='windows-1251')
```

And I get the string that I need.

Is it a bug? Or am I doing it wrong?

Python 3.6.0 (default, Jan 16 2017, 12:12:55) 
[GCC 6.3.1 20170109] on linux
---
aiohttp==2.0.3
```

issue_title: web.View didn't support multi inheritance init chain
issue_body:
## Long story short

I am using class based view, when creating my own base handler class inherited from web.View and other CommonMixin class,  the ``__init__`` chain call broken due to web.View and abc.AbstractView didn't use ``super().__init__(request)``

```python3
from aiohttp.web import View

class CommonHandler(object):
    def __init__(self, req):
         super().__init__()
         print('common init done')

class BaseHandler(View, CommonHandler):
    def __init__(self, req):
        super().__init__(req)

hdl = BaseHandler(None)
```
## Expected behaviour
output `common init done`


## Actual behaviour
no output, ``CommonHandler.__init__()`` didn't called.

## Steps to reproduce

above code

## Your environment
linux
python 3.5.2
``aiohttp.__version__`` 1.3.3

I used this patch to fix it:
```diff
diff --git a/aiohttp/abc.py b/aiohttp/abc.py
index dc34325..256f3c7 100644
--- a/aiohttp/abc.py
+++ b/aiohttp/abc.py
@@ -81,6 +81,7 @@ class AbstractMatchInfo(ABC):
 class AbstractView(ABC):
 
     def __init__(self, request):
+        super().__init__(request)
         self._request = request
 
     @property
diff --git a/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py b/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py
index c8b0519..b78c6cb 100644
--- a/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py
+++ b/aiohttp/web_urldispatcher.py
@@ -646,6 +646,9 @@ class SystemRoute(AbstractRoute):
 
 class View(AbstractView):
 
+    def __init__(self, request):
+        super().__init__(request)
+
     @asyncio.coroutine
     def __iter__(self):
         if self.request._method not in hdrs.METH_ALL:
```

issue_title: Avoid inconsistencies when notebook is opened in more than one window
issue_body:
It is sometimes useful to open the same notebook in two windows (e.g. to have a look at code in an earlier cell whilst writing code in a new cell). However, if the user is not careful, they may make changes in either window which cannot easily be reconciled. 

It would be great if we could introduce a mechanism to avoid such inconsistencies (beyond people like myself just being more careful). For example, we could display a modal window before allowing editing in all but the most recently opened window. Looking forward to comments and other ideas.

issue_title: Add support to Bookmarks
issue_body:
This is a feature request. Most file browsers nowadays have bookmarks that allow user to directly jump to a directory without going through the tree. (at least in Nautilus / Windows Explorer)

Such feature can be quite useful when a user works on many projects in various locations of the file system, because a bookmark will allow the user to quickly jump to the project and set up a 'contextual' workspace, without clicking through several layers of directory hierarchy.

Bookmarks will also allow the user to jump to a directory that's off the 'root' of the notebook server. This is useful in some setups of jupyterhub where there users also obtain a separate location for storing files in addition to their home directory. 

To minimal changes to the UX is to append bookmarks to the 'Running'  page. halted notebooks and directories can be appended to the list, probably with a gray icon to indicate they are bookmarks to inactive notebook or a folder icon to indicate it is an icon.

Bookmarks shall be broken if the original location is gone.

issue_title: Make Model implement MutableMapping
issue_body:
The `Model` class has all the methods defined in [collections.abc.MutableMapping](https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/collections.abc.html), but doesn't actually implement it:
```python
>>> from collections.abc import *
>>> from schematics.models import Model
>>> class Foo(Model): pass
... 
>>> mutable_mapping_attrs = ('__getitem__', '__setitem__', '__delitem__', '__iter__', '__len__')
>>> all(hasattr(Foo(), attr) for attr in mutable_mapping_attrs)
True
>>> isinstance(Foo(), MutableMapping)
False
```

Adding `MutableMapping` to the base classes of `Model` would provide automatic implementations for usual dict methods such as `update`, which would be quite nice since one would expect a `Model`'s instance to behave exactly like a dict since it "looks like it and quacks like it".

issue_title: Differentiation with np.select (piecewise functions)
issue_body:
You're doing some great work here! I've run into a slight issue with computing gradients of piecewise functions using `np.select`. I've put together a minimal (trivial) example (Python 3.3):

```python
import autograd.numpy as anp
from autograd import grad

def func(x):
    return anp.select([True], [x[0]*(anp.log(x[1]**x[1]) + anp.log(x[2]**x[2]))])

gradient = grad(func)(anp.array([300., 0.5, 0.5]))
print(gradient)
```
```
[ 0.  0.  0.]
[...]/autograd/core.py:39: UserWarning: Output seems independent of input. Returning zero gradient.
  warnings.warn("Output seems independent of input. Returning zero gradient.")
```

`select` accepts a list of conditions and a list of choices, and the first condition to be evaluated `True` is returned from the corresponding choice list. The gradient of a piecewise-defined function should just be the gradient of the choice list, but I haven't yet wrapped my head around how constructing a closure for `defgrad` would work in this context.

issue_title: Modular visualization JavaScript in the HTML file or separate?
issue_body:
Right now, the JavaScript to render each visualization module is included in the template HTML file as a script tag. This means that on the final page, each visualization div has its own separate script tag. This is ugly, and also not very standard for web development. But none of the alternatives are great:

- Include the code in a separate JavaScript template. This means that each module needs at least two external files: an HTML template, and a JavaScript template.

- Have users write template code directly in Python, as a string. This seems non-ideal, since it involves writing JavaScript in a Python environment, as a long and hard-to-debug string.

- Other ideas?

issue_title: xfail mark ignored if xdist slave process crashes
issue_body:
When a test causes an xdist slave-worker process to crash, the test always reports a failure, even if that test is has the `xfail` mark. It seems preferable to keep track of `xfail` marks in the master, and report an `xfail` rather than a `FAIL` for a test that crashed a slave.

Obviously it's better if we can write our tests to avoid crashing the slave process, but that's not always possible. Since xdist ignores the xfail mark on crashed tests, there's no way to communicate that such a test is allowed to fail. It also breaks symmetry with the `XPASS` and `xfail` statuses reported when such tests don't crash.

Sample tests in *test_foo.py*:
```python
import os
import pytest

@pytest.mark.xfail
def test_bar():
    assert False

@pytest.mark.xfail
def test_baz():
    os.execv('/bin/sh', ['-c', 'exit 1'])
```

Sample run output:

```
$ pytest -v -n1
========================================= test session starts ==========================================
platform linux -- Python 3.6.3, pytest-3.3.1, py-1.5.2, pluggy-0.6.0 -- /home/nick/.local/share/virtualenvs/xdist-crash-x3F5dNZR/bin/python3.6m
cachedir: .cache
rootdir: /home/nick/stuff/xdist-crash, inifile:
plugins: xdist-1.20.1, forked-0.2
[gw0] linux Python 3.6.3 cwd: /home/nick/stuff/xdist-crash
[gw0] Python 3.6.3 (default, Oct  6 2017, 08:44:35)  -- [GCC 5.4.0 20160609]
gw0 [2]
scheduling tests via LoadScheduling

test_foo.py::test_bar
[gw0] [ 50%] xfail test_foo.py::test_bar
test_foo.py::test_baz
[gw0] node down: Not properly terminated
[gw0] [100%] FAILED test_foo.py::test_baz
Replacing crashed slave gw0
[gw1] linux Python 3.6.3 cwd: /home/nick/stuff/xdist-crash
[gw1] Python 3.6.3 (default, Oct  6 2017, 08:44:35)  -- [GCC 5.4.0 20160609]
gw1 ok
=============================================== FAILURES ===============================================
_____________________________________________ test_foo.py ______________________________________________
[gw0] linux -- Python 3.6.3 /home/nick/.local/share/virtualenvs/xdist-crash-x3F5dNZR/bin/python3.6m
Slave 'gw0' crashed while running 'test_foo.py::test_baz'

issue_title: Session-Scoped Fixtures are not Session-Scoped with Pytest-Xdist
issue_body:
I am fairly new to this project. I very recently migrated a software project test-suite from nosetest to pytest, mainly because of the Xdist benefits I had heard of.

The problem is that my Tests are depending on a big fixture setup (table creation + heavy loading of data) that I would like to share across *all* my tests.

The current Xdist behaviour is as follow:
1. Collect test
2. Split tests amongst the user-defined number of processes
3. Launch a pytest session for each worker

Obviously, if each test depend on a heavy fixture, then multiplying the number of creation per number of worker is not going to help.

Additionally, it simply breaks the expected pytest behaviour for 'session' scoped fixtures.

I think this should be fairly simple to address this problem although I didn't take a really deep look into it. If you need help to solve this problem, I am more than willing to contribute if you feel this suggestion for improvement is relevant.

Greetings.

issue_title: netstandard2.0 library, dependent on Microsoft.Win32.Registry, doesn't work from net471 when loaded via MEF
issue_body:
# Issue Title

I'm building a .net standard 2.0 library that uses MEF to load extensions. The MEF parts are also .net standard 2.0.

Everything works fine when called from netcore2.0 test project. But if i have the test project also target net471, calls to Microsoft.Win32.Registry from the MEF part fail (Registry.LocalMachine returns null). I assume this is because Microsoft.Win32.Registry has a net461-specific library that isn't present.

I see this problem doesn't exist if, instead of using MEF, i just directly depend on the part. I imagine MEF prevents the framework-specific dependencies from flowing through correctly. Any suggestions?

# General

You can repro this by cloning https://github.com/jherby2k/AudioWorks.git then changing the AudioWorks.Api.Tests projects to also target net471. Tests using the AudioWorks.Extensions.Apple extension will fail because it can't read the registry.

http://blog.stevensanderson.com/2016/10/04/angular2-template-for-visual-studio/

That mentions ".Net Core 1.0.1" and the link for that takes me here:

https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/dotnet/2016/09/13/announcing-september-2016-updates-for-net-core-1-0/

That page talks a lot about installing ".Net Core 1.0.1" but seems to have no helpful Download link!!

Searching for downloads of .Net Core gets me here:

https://www.microsoft.com/net/download/core

But that page refers to ".Net Core 1.0.3 SDK"? Excuse me? SDK, Tools, erm what's going on here?

So utterly confused, I visit this page next:

https://www.microsoft.com/net/core#windowsvs2015

That doesn't mention ".Net Core 1.0.3 SDK" but rather ".Net Core 1.0.1 tools Preview 2".

Sorry Microsoft this make all make sense to those deeply involved in these emerging technologies, but you're all doing a terrible job at actually explaining all this.

So far I see these "product" mentioned by Microsoft:

".Net Core 1.0.1"
".Net Core 1.0.1 tools Preview 2"
".Net Core 1.0.3 SDK".

Don't you agree that setting up a page that collects all of .Net Core downloads into a single place, with a brief summary of what each one is would be nice for all the developers who are keen to explore what you've been doing?

How hard is that? a ".Net Core Downloads" page with every published/released installable product mentioned and summarized?

issue_title: IL Linker does not remove unused classes / methods
issue_body:
It is stated [here](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/samples/linker-instructions.md) that
> The linker removes code in your application and dependent libraries that are not reached by any code paths

I am following [these instructions](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/samples/linker-instructions.md#instructions). Before publishing the app I add unused class to the project:
```
echo internal class UnusedClass { private class UnusedNestedClass { private void UnusedMethod() { System.Console.WriteLine("This method is never called.");}}}> UnusedClass.cs
```
To make sure the linker processes the main assembly add these lines to the `testapp.csproj` file:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
    <ExtraLinkerArgs>-c link -u link -t -l none -b true --skip-unresolved true --verbose</ExtraLinkerArgs>
</PropertyGroup>
```
My publish command:
```
dotnet publish -c release -r win-x64 -o out /p:ShowLinkerSizeComparison=true
```
After publishing decompile `out/testapp.dll` (I use ILSpy).

_Expected_: UnusedClass is removed.
_Actual_: UnusedClass is still there.

**dotnet --version**: 2.1.300-preview2-008533
**IL Linker** from [this package](https://dotnet.myget.org/feed/dotnet-core/package/nuget/ILLink.Tasks/0.1.5-preview-1461378)

issue_title: button values?
issue_body: Currently html_text doesn't extract text from button values (e.g. ``<input type="submit" value="Send">`` ). It may be nice to have them extracted, at least optionally - they may be helpful as features for ML algorithms, and they look like text. But I don't know how important is this feature :)

issue_title: Rename the auth command
issue_body: Rename the auth command to something more meaningful. Auth might make users expect to have to create an account or to login to a service provider. Make it certonly.

issue_title: Suppressing librdkafka log-messages completely and efficient ?
issue_body:
Description
===========
Hello,

i got some questions about suppressing log messages caused by librdkafka, please follow along the events:

Like in  @https://github.com/edenhill/librdkafka/issues/437 i see log messages from underlying librdkafka:

`1507111970.888|ERROR|rdkafka#consumer-11| <some_IP>:9092/49: Receive failed: Disconnected`

which i successfully suppress by using `'log.connection.close': 'false'`. Shortly after the above occures i see messages like these in my logs:

`1507111970.888|ERROR|rdkafka#consumer-11| 4/4 brokers are down`

As far as i understood this is a logical consequence. Since the producer is out of messages, let´s say "drained" it will loosely disconnect the consumers and notice them if there are new messages to consume.

Since the message is not important to me, i tried to suppress them like @https://github.com/edenhill/librdkafka/issues/915 suggests by setting `topic.metadata.refresh.interval.ms` to an interval lower than `connection.max.idle.ms`. Which i suppose (i do not have access to the Kafka instance nor configs) is about `300000 ms` .

This only does the job half-way because these messages still pop up every now and then. Also i wonder if this is the best way to solve the problem. Like @https://github.com/edenhill/librdkafka/issues/437#issuecomment-170458437 states, heartbeating is a costly thing.

Is there a better way to handle this behaviour which suppresses the messages and also is not that costly ?

N/A

issue_title: Parser should set `fold` attribute in ambiguous times with time zone information if possible
issue_body:
Running this with the TZ environment set to `US/Eastern`:

```python
>>> from dateutil import parser, tz
>>> dt = parser.parse('2011-11-06 01:30:00 EST')
>>> dt.tzname()
'EDT'
>>> tz.enfold(dt, fold=1).tzname()
'EST'
```

This is a situation where we start with an unambiguous date but because of how the `tzlocal()` parsing works, end up with an ambiguous date. Right now this mainly affects `tzlocal()` but it may also be useful in this situation:

```python
>>> from dateutil import parser, tz
>>> tzinfos = {'EDT': tz.gettz('US/Eastern'), 'EST': tz.gettz('US/Eastern')}
>>> dt = parser.parse('2011-11-06 01:03:00 EST', tzinfos=tzinfos)
>>> dt.tzname()
'EDT'
```

This last one is dubious because it's not guaranteed that the `tzname` is going to match one of the keywords, but it's probably worth trying when the date is ambiguous, just in case. That said, if you're specifying `tzinfos`, you might as well just specify `tzoffset` values and then use `.astimezone()` to convert to whatever time zone you actually want to be in.

issue_title: rrulestr Generates Datetime with Incorrect Daylight Savings Value
issue_body:
I'm using the dateutil 2.4.3 and encountering the following error using `rrulestr`:

```python
import pytz
from datetime import datetime
from dateutil.rrule import rrulestr

pacific_tz = pytz.timezone('America/Los_Angeles')
rrule_string = 'RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;COUNT=2;BYDAY=MO'
dt_start = pacific_tz.localize(datetime(2014, 10, 27, 9, 0))
```
 `list(rrulestr(rrule_string, dtstart=dt_start))` outputs the following:
```python
datetime.datetime(2014, 10, 27, 9, 0, tzinfo=<DstTzInfo 'America/Los_Angeles' PDT-1 day, 17:00:00 DST>), 
datetime.datetime(2014, 11, 3, 9, 0, tzinfo=<DstTzInfo 'America/Los_Angeles' PDT-1 day, 17:00:00 DST>)]
```

The second datetime is incorrect:

`datetime.datetime(2014, 11, 3, 9, 0, tzinfo=<DstTzInfo 'America/Los_Angeles' PDT-1 day, 17:00:00 DST>)` --> November 3rd 9AM should not be in DST.

The *correct* datetime would be:
```python
pytz.timezone('America/Los_Angeles').localize(datetime(2014, 11, 3, 9, 0))
datetime.datetime(2014, 11, 3, 9, 0, tzinfo=<DstTzInfo 'America/Los_Angeles' PST-1 day, 16:00:00 STD>)

```

issue_title: Issue with tests involving response_helpers due to invalid docstrings
issue_body:
I was struck by this while working on https://github.com/marshmallow-code/apispec/pull/219. But I think the test failure is not due to the change in the plugin interface.

I wonder how this feature is supposed to work. Maybe a test is missing.

The code in core.py reads:

```python
        # Process response helpers for any path operations defined.
        # Rule is that method + http status exist in both operations and helpers
        methods = set(iterkeys(path.operations)) & set(iterkeys(self._response_helpers))
        for method in methods:
            responses = path.operations[method]['responses']
            statuses = set(iterkeys(responses)) & set(iterkeys(self._response_helpers[method]))
            for status_code in statuses:
                for func in self._response_helpers[method][status_code]:
                    responses[status_code].update(
                        func(self, **kwargs)
                    )
```

I think the issue here is that it assumes `responses[status_code]` is a `dict`, which it may not be. In this example, it comes as a string `'description:some data'`.

I've pushed a branch with a [failing test](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/apispec/commit/5139b89fd4131442e89992b3d6bc1b05d6360a2a) showing this.

I'm not sure what to think about this. Perhaps the issue is in the step before, the path parser that fails to parse the YAML docstring describing the operation response.

I'm a bit confused. I don't use YAML docstrings in my code, as I use another extension to pass everything as `dict`, so this is not the part that I know best. Hence this pretty blurry question.

Filing this here for now. It kind of blocks https://github.com/marshmallow-code/apispec/pull/219 unless I either modify the test to remove offending feature, or modify the code to introduce a clumsy logic so as to avoid triggering this.

issue_title: optim state has no clear specifications and prone to errors
issue_body:
This is an issue for both scala and python. 
Use python as example:
```python
state = {"batchSize": batch_size,
         "learningRate": lr,
         "learningRateDecay": lrd}
    #configure optimizer
    optimizer = Optimizer(
        model=model,
        training_rdd=train_rdd,
        criterion=ClassNLLCriterion(),
        end_trigger=MaxEpoch(max_epoch),
        batch_size=batch_size,
        optim_method=optim,
        state=state) 
```
Problems include:
1) User can set any (key,value) into state without receiving any warning or error message. Our program just silently ignore any illegal states. If user misspelled the name of a key, he won't know that it never takes effect (unless the state is tracked in tensorboard or somewhere). 
1) Different optim method may just enable a subset of state parameters, and some of the parameters may override each other or take effects together. e.g. learningRateDecay and Step. Need more clear specifications of which params are enabled and how they take effect for each optim method. 

Need a checking for illegal params, and docs about the params in optim method.

issue_title: Customizing relationships
issue_body:
Problem

`extract_relationships` should be able to handle specialized cases, like allowing a `links` or `meta` object instead of a `data` object. For example, in our application it needs to: 1. recognize other field types as relationships 2. return a `links` object instead of a `data` object with type/id The relationship data is read-only.

Desired

![screen shot 2015-08-04 at 3 28 42 pm](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/9755598/9071636/599be0c6-3ac4-11e5-9778-1983ffe75510.png)

Potential solutions

@jerel suggested a serializer hook like `add_relationships` for customization. According to the spec, under `relationships` you must include a `links`, `data`, or `meta` object. http://jsonapi.org/format/#document-resource-object-relationships. The current `extract_relationships` just allows for a `data` object. It would be valuable for users to be able to have a `links` object, instead.

issue_title: Question about apply_momentum function in Lasagne
issue_body:
```
def apply_momentum(updates, params=None, momentum=0.9):
    """Returns a modified update dictionary including momentum

    Generates update expressions of the form:

    * ``velocity := momentum * velocity + updates[param] - param``
    * ``param := param + velocity``

    Parameters
    ----------
    updates : OrderedDict
        A dictionary mapping parameters to update expressions
    params : iterable of shared variables, optional
        The variables to apply momentum to. If omitted, will apply
        momentum to all `updates.keys()`.
    momentum : float or symbolic scalar, optional
        The amount of momentum to apply. Higher momentum results in
        smoothing over more update steps. Defaults to 0.9.

    Returns
    -------
    OrderedDict
        A copy of `updates` with momentum updates for all `params`.

    Notes
    -----
    Higher momentum also results in larger update steps. To counter that,
    you can optionally scale your learning rate by `1 - momentum`.

    See Also
    --------
    momentum : Shortcut applying momentum to SGD updates
    """
    if params is None:
        params = updates.keys()
    updates = OrderedDict(updates)

    for param in params:
        value = param.get_value(borrow=True)
        velocity = theano.shared(np.zeros(value.shape, dtype=value.dtype),
                                 broadcastable=param.broadcastable)
        x = momentum * velocity + updates[param]
        updates[velocity] = x - param
        updates[param] = x

    return updates
```
In 
```
for param in params:
        value = param.get_value(borrow=True)
        velocity = theano.shared(np.zeros(value.shape, dtype=value.dtype),
                                 broadcastable=param.broadcastable)
        x = momentum * velocity + updates[param]
        updates[velocity] = x - param
        updates[param] = x
```
My question is about the velocity variable, In my opinion, every param should have a distinct velocity,
Is it legal that all of them have the same name?

issue_title: Django 1.8 and Translations
issue_body:
I monkey patched the pull request in #114, however I ran into another issue when translations are getting deferred: In Django 1.8 `translation.get_translation()` has changed to return None when translations are being deferred ([link](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.8/ref/utils/#django.utils.translation.get_language)), and in my case, line 105 of models/fields.py 

```
locale = translation.to_locale(translation.get_language())
```

was throwing the error: _AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'find'_ since `translation.get_language()` was returning None with the new functionality. I fixed my local version the following way, however this seems pretty incorrect in the grand scheme of things

```
language = translation.get_language() or settings.LANGUAGE_CODE
locale = translation.to_locale(language)
```

I don't really know the proper way to fix this, so leaving here and hopefully someone with more knowledge of both code-bases can fix = )

issue_title: Create a troubleshooting guide
issue_body:
Largely speaking, the most consistent problem we run into is with kernels spawning (we have a lot of related issues for this). While we double down on fixing the core issues, we should also have a semi-handy guide (`TROUBLESHOOTING.md`) to help us help others.

Here's a starter:

## ERROR_EXECUTING Kernel not connected

* Open up the dev tools
* Report any errors seen
* Install the python kernel with `python -m ipykernel install --user`
* Fully restart nteract

issue_title: Support remote Postgres instances
issue_body:
Unless I missing something, Odo currently relies on custom SQL via SQLAlchemy to execute a COPY statement that reads/writes from files local to the server. This may be disabled on some instances (eg AWS RDS), or not possible in a distributed environment where code can't run on the same instance as the source/target database.
https://github.com/blaze/odo/blob/master/odo/backends/sql_csv.py#L154
https://github.com/blaze/odo/blob/master/odo/backends/sql.py#L696

The Postgres COPY statement also supports reading/writing from stdin/stdout, which allows bulk reads/writes without requiring access to the local file system. The psycodb2 driver implements this through the cursor.copy_from/copy_to methods.

I'm not sure how to access this without going direct to the driver - eg not using SQLAlchemy - but doing so would allow Odo to be used in a much wider number of use cases (such as ours, where we have an ETL process bulk loading multiple different sources into a single target Postgres instance on AWS RDS.)

issue_title: wechatpy 2.0 的一些想法
issue_body:
wehctapy 已经在我们的项目中用了几个月，确实方便了我们很多。

但也有些用的别扭的地方。慢慢的也产生了一些想法。希望和你交流一下。


**出现此想法的原因**

- 在项目中使用时，感觉现有些接口比较难用。
- 接口返回数据是纯 json，而非对象
- 只支持官方操作
- 不够对象化
- 发布时非接口类功能缺失
- 没有对接 python 常用框架

改进方向：`易用性`、`功能性`

**接口返回数据是纯 json，而非对象**

这在项目中还要做各种处理，这种处理放在业务中意义不大，浪费精力，适合放到本库中。提供一下接口会更好：
做法：将微信中的概念变成对象，将接口变成对象的方法，方法返回对象实例。对象实例提供各种数据格式的转化能力。
```python
follower_manager =   wechat_client.get_manager('fellower')
# 获取所有关注者
followers = follower_manager.get()
# 获取单个关注者
follower =   wechat_client.fellowers.get(name='nickname')
# 修改
follower.nickname = 'another_name'
follower.sex = ’女'
follower.save()

shakearound_manager =   wechat_client.get_manager('shakearound')
page_manager = shakearound_manager.get_manager('page')
all_pages = page_manager.get()
page = page_manager.get(page_id)
new_page = page_manager.add(title, description, url, icon_url, mark='')
new_page.json()
new_page.xml()
```

**只支持官方操作**

可以在官方操作的基础上封装常用操作，这样更方便使用


**不够对象化**
```python
wechat_client = WechatClient(app_id, app_secret)
```
实例化之后，就可以检查wechat_client可调用的对象（底层接口），没有权限时，抛出异常；
所有操作以面向对象的方式使用，屏蔽 http 层。

**发布时非接口类功能缺失**

发布项目时，经常需要额外开发。比如验证 token，开放平台的基本测试等，可以在库中实现。节省调用者开发时间。


**没有对接 python 常用框架**

多数情况下微信对象需要本地保存，如果能对象 django ORM、SqlAlchemy ORM 等数据库模型，会大大增加我们项目的友好度。比如 Django 中
```python
from wechatpy.decorators import bind_model
from wechatpy.models import Fellower
from django.db import models

@bind_model(Fellower)
class WechatFellower(models.Model):
     name = models.CharField(...)
```
这样就把 wechatpy 中的模型和 Django 的模型关联了。
```python
wechat_fellower = WechatFellower.objects.get(name='xxx')
wechat_fellower.name = 'another_name'
wechat_fellower.save()
```
在完成 django 模型功能（保存到数据库）的同时，也完成了和微信接口的交互。

issue_title: Add support for IPv6 Zone Identifiers
issue_body:
Due to hyperlink using `socket.inet_pton()` to parse IPv6, IPv6 zone identifiers aren't supported. 

```
>>> hyperlink.URL.from_text(u'https://[fe80:3438:7667:5c77:ce27%eth0]')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "hyperlink/_url.py", line 1135, in from_text
    rooted, userinfo, uses_netloc)
  File "hyperlink/_url.py", line 797, in __init__
    _, self._host = parse_host(_textcheck('host', host, '/?#@'))
  File "hyperlink/_url.py", line 697, in parse_host
    raise URLParseError('invalid IPv6 host: %r (%r)' % (host, se))
hyperlink._url.URLParseError: invalid IPv6 host: u'fe80:3438:7667:5c77:ce27%eth0' (error('illegal IP address string passed to inet_pton',))
```

While `inet_pton()` may not support them, other socket module functions do. This is probably best addressed by switching to regex-based parsing of hosts, which also happens to address hyperlink's Windows-specific branch (socket module on Windows Py2 doesn't support inet_pton).

issue_title: outputSE option for DIRAC jobs
issue_body:
I'm planning to add an ```outputSE``` option to the ```Dirac``` backend for DIRAC jobs in 6.1.20.

This will only become a requirement when a user tries to submit a job that requires outputdata but if a user attempts to submit a job without requesting an outputSE then the job submission should fail.

There is currently no nice automated way to upload data automatically to a writable SE based upon where a user can currently write to.

This will be independent of work on the ```DiracFile``` to make it work in a more vanilla DIRAC friendly way.

Obviously I'll add/change some options for LHCb so that LHCb users are unaffected by this change.

Are there any objections to this?

issue_title: Logging cache flushing too early
issue_body:
The logging Memory cache is flushing on every single return statement.

This can be quite annoying if entering say a for loop command whilst the monitoring is busy.

The expected behaviour is that the logging only be flushed on the finishing of an input statement in IPython.

I think we can achieve this without having a custom input parser again, as I think there is a hook for post-input in their API. If this is a 1 line fix I'll look into it.

issue_title: The documentation for running in production stores the kinto.sock in a tmpfs
issue_body:
Following the configuration for [running in production with uwsgi](http://kinto.readthedocs.org/en/latest/configuration/production.html#running-with-uwsgi), it advises to store the `kinto.sock` file in `/var/run/uwsgi/kinto.sock` which, at least on some distributions, is a tmpfs (temporary filesystem).

This makes uwsgi complain: `bind(): Permission denied [core/socket.c line 185]`

See also http://stackoverflow.com/questions/12778646/uwsgi-socket-permissions

issue_title: retitle javascript.rst "Guide to implementing a client"
issue_body: The `javascript.rst` tutorial in this documentation is basically a low-level guide to implementing a client. I don't think it's useful considering the `HTTPie` examples strewn throughout the documentation, but if we're going to keep it, we should make it extremely clear that this code is meant for library developers and NOT for JavaScript application developers.

issue_title: Return 400 if a group contains system.Everyone or a group URL
issue_body:
Unless I'm mistaken:

* We don't support groups for anonymous requests
* We don't support recursivity in groups definitions

So we should reject with `400` if such groups definitons are created

issue_title: Add a configuration of collections that the history plugin needs to keep track on
issue_body:
Today the history plugin applies to all the collection but most of them don't need it.
For instance with the kinto-signer plugin we don't want to track history of changes in the preview and signed collection.
The same goes with the kinto-changes plugin when we don't want to track monitor changes modifications.

The same way we can configure the kinto-signer resources we want to track, we should be able to configure the list of collections we want the history plugin to track.

issue_title: Add some Splash specific retry logic to scrapy-splash
issue_body: When Splash returns some error it is just retried by Scrapy without printing error messages. This is a pain because if you get 400 from Splash you should  be able to see error message immediately, usually 400 means some error from your side so you must know what you did wrong. WIth current middleware you just get 400 in logs and usually this is retried by Scrapy retry middleware, there is no quick and easy way to see what error you got.

issue_title: Please fix asymmetry in rs::context notifications about devices being added / removed
issue_body:
* Before opening a new issue, we wanted to provide you with some useful suggestions (Click "Preview" above for a better view):

    * Consider checking out SDK [examples](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/examples#sample-code-for-intel-realsense-cameras).
    * Have you looked in our [documentations](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/doc#useful-links)?
    * Is you question a [frequently asked one](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/wiki/Troubleshooting-Q%26A)?
    * Try [searching our GitHub Issues](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) (open and closed) for a similar issue.

* All users are welcomed to report bugs, ask questions, suggest or request enhancements and generally feel free to open new issue, even if they haven't followed any of the suggestions above :)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D400 series (D400, D415, D435) | 
| Firmware Version                   | N/A | 
| Operating System & Version |   All  | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  All                                         | 
| Platform                                 | All  |
| SDK Version                            |  Master as of 719b0b95cdfd6a5c9a3a07e34bea48fce5f35d2a                          | 
| Language                            |  C++, Python                         | 
| Segment			|  All                          | 

### Issue Description
The C++ (and, consequently, python API which is what I actually care about) for notifications when devices are added / removed is asymmetric. The events expose a list of newly added devices but force you to query for the removal of specific devices. I would like these to be made symmetric in the following ways:

1. Add a method like `bool was_added(const rs2::device& dev) const` to see if a specific device has been re-added (e.g. after issuing a software reset to the device). The signature may need to change (e.g. instead of assuming you have a live device handle, support doing this by serial number maybe since the handles must have potentially been invalidated by the reset event)

2. Add a method like `device_list get_removed_devices() const`

3. Update all the bindings to reflect these changes please :-)

issue_title: Modify the TensorFlow Scheduler and Runtime to Change the Operations Priority
issue_body:
Hi,

I am trying to modify the TensorFlow scheduler and runtime to change the operation priorities. As my understanding, TensorFlow has inter-operation and intra-operation thread pool with a scheduler scheduling operations for different threads and there is also a FIFO queue of operations for operations waiting. The workflow between them is that operations are sent to the inter-op thread pool from the executor, and then that work is running through XLA compiler to eventually be executed on the intra-op thread pool. Schedule() is in inside a specific ThreadPool and is to schedule function for the threads in the pool. The thread scheduler selects a subset of threads to run at any given moment. When the tasks are passed to the ThreadPool, they are added to one of the scheduler thread’s FIFO queues and then the scheduler will pick up the tasks distributed to the available worker threads. I have all the control and data dependencies got from the Graph in TensorFlow by using TensorBoard. Now I am not sure whether my understanding for the TensorFlow scheduler and runtime is correct or not.

The problem is that I still have no clue how and where to modify the threadpool/scheduler/ready_queue or others to change the operations priority/sequence for different threads at the TensorFlow runtime by modifying the source code. Does anyone have any ideas?

issue_title: Problem about Dataset Delay Timer Management
issue_body:
Hello,

When I was trying the harness certificate test in NCP mode:
9.2.18 RollingBackTheAciveTimestampWithPendingCommissioningDataset(Router and ED).
I got failure results and then I did some analysis, I guess I found a problem about the Dataset Delay Timer Management, 

According to the specification(8.4.3.4 Delay Timer Management):
When a Thread Device's Delay Timer reaches zero, the Thread Device compares Active Operational Dataset Active Timestamp with the Pending Operational Dataset Active Timestamp. If the Pending Operational Dataset Active Timestamp is newer **or the Pending Operational Dataset contains a new Network Master Key**, the Thread Device MUST:
1. Replace the Active Operational Dataset with the Pending Operational Dataset, removing the Delay Timer and Pending Timestamp at the same time.
2. Commit the new Active Operational Dataset to non-volatile memory.
3. Completely remove the Pending Operational Dataset.

The case 9.2.18 uses a new Network Master Key(00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ee) in pending dataset, so it is supposed that the pending operational dataset will take effect after delay timer timeout.

However, I checked the API otError DatasetManager::Set(const Dataset &aDataset)
https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/master/src/core/meshcop/dataset_manager.cpp#L147
I found that it only cares about the timestamp, but doesn't take masterkey into consideration.

The result is, before the delay timer timeout, pending dataset is:
Thread:PendingDataset = [
        "Dataset:ActiveTimestamp          =  0x0000000000000014"
        "Dataset:PendingTimestamp         =  0x0000000000000014"
        "Dataset:Channel                  =  15"
        "Dataset:NetworkName              =  "MyHouse""
        "Dataset:PanId                    =  0xFACE"
        "Dataset:ExtendedPanId            =  0x000DB8000000"
        "Dataset:MasterKey                =  [00112233445566778899AABBCCDDEE**EE**]"
        "Dataset:MeshLocalPrefix          =  fd00:db8::/64"
        "Dataset:Delay                    =  19198"
        "Dataset:ChannelMaskPage0         =  0x07FFF800"
        "Dataset:PSKc                     =  [10B95765596AB9D0B86CEBDD0FA24DA3]"
        "Dataset:SecPolicy:KeyRotation    =  3600"
        "Dataset:SecPolicy:Flags          =  0xF8"
]

After delay timer timeout,  the pending dataset is removed but active dataset is still unchanged.
wpanctl:wpan0> get Thread:PendingDataset
Thread:PendingDataset = []
wpanctl:wpan0> get Thread:ActiveDataset
Thread:ActiveDataset = [
        "Dataset:ActiveTimestamp          =  0x0000000000004E20"
        "Dataset:Channel                  =  15"
        "Dataset:NetworkName              =  "GRL""
        "Dataset:PanId                    =  0xFACE"
        "Dataset:ExtendedPanId            =  0x000DB8000000"
        "Dataset:MasterKey                =  [00112233445566778899AABBCCDDEE**FF**]"
        "Dataset:MeshLocalPrefix          =  fd00:db8::/64"
        "Dataset:ChannelMaskPage0         =  0x07FFF800"
        "Dataset:PSKc                     =  [10B95765596AB9D0B86CEBDD0FA24DA3]"
        "Dataset:SecPolicy:KeyRotation    =  3600"
        "Dataset:SecPolicy:Flags          =  0xF8"
]

In my opinion, it is against the specification, active dataset should be replaced by pending dataset, hope to know your thoughts about this.
Thanks!

issue_title: [mle] Ensure that the radio is left in the receive State while Thread is stopped and the Interface is up.
issue_body: Currently, when Thread is stopped the radio is transitioned to sleep.  This is technically a bug as the radio should transition to the receive State until the Interface is brought down.  Ideally, the code that Thread uses to transition the radio into and out of Sleep should be separate from the code used when Thread is stopped.  Furthermore, the code should avoid toggling the radio state while Thread is stopped if possible.

issue_title: Confusion Matrix for HDF5 Convolutional Neural Network
issue_body:
I am currently working with TFLearn to test different CNNs on a dataset. I have converted the dataset to HDF5 and managed to train a few practice networks. My question is how do I predict and plot a confusion matrix? The code I have so far is as follows:

```
from __future__ import division, print_function, absolute_import

import tflearn
from tflearn.layers.core import input_data, dropout, fully_connected
from tflearn.layers.conv import conv_2d, max_pool_2d
from tflearn.layers.normalization import local_response_normalization
from tflearn.layers.estimator import regression

import h5py
import numpy
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix

# Import test data
hdf5Test = h5py.File('/home/fletch67/dfhProject/Data/twoClasses/twoTest.h5',
                      'r')

# Split into features and labels
X = hdf5Test['X']
Y = hdf5Test['Y']

# Building convolutional network
network = input_data(shape=[None, 240, 320, 3], name='input')
network = conv_2d(network, 32, 3, activation='relu', regularizer="L2")
network = max_pool_2d(network, 2)
network = local_response_normalization(network)
network = conv_2d(network, 64, 3, activation='relu', regularizer="L2")
network = max_pool_2d(network, 2)
network = local_response_normalization(network)
network = fully_connected(network, 128, activation='tanh')
network = dropout(network, 0.8)
network = fully_connected(network, 256, activation='tanh')
network = dropout(network, 0.8)
network = fully_connected(network, 2, activation='softmax')
network = regression(
  network,
  optimizer='sgd',
  learning_rate=0.01,
  loss='categorical_crossentropy',
  name='target'
)

# Training
model = tflearn.DNN(network, tensorboard_verbose=0)
model.load('/home/fletch67/dfhProject/Models/twoClasses/basicCNN/basicCNN_test'
           '.tflearn')

# Predict Values

print(predictions)
```

When ever I try to print predictions I get the following error:

Aborted (core dumped)
```

I've read that sklearn can be used to produce a confusion matrix, however, have been unable to find direct instructions, any help or guidance would be appreciated.

issue_title: Enhancement: allow method definition with python code by an augmenting meta class
issue_body:
## Issue description

Making it easy to define new methods for pybind11 generated classes, in Python code instead of in C++ code, and without actually creating new subclasses.

## Reproducible example code

I come across this enhancement implemented by a meta class:
```python
from . import core

# must derive from pybind11's default meta class
class AugmentCxxClass(core.PerfPack.__class__):
    """
    Make it easy to define new methods for pybind11 generated classes, in Python code instead of in C++ code,
    and without actually creating new subclasses.
    """

    def __new__(cls, name, bases, namespace, **kwds):
        assert len(bases) == 1, 'Augment multiple classes at same time ?!'
        for k, v in namespace.items():
            if k in ('__module__', '__qualname__'):
                continue
            for tcls in bases:
                setattr(tcls, k, v)
        return bases[0]
```

Then for my bound C++ class `Freq`, I can neatly augment it with python code and re-export it from the outer python module:
```python
class Freq(core.Freq, metaclass=AugmentCxxClass):
    # preserve generated docstring by not defining one here

    def __repr__(self):
        return f'as_freq({self.nomenclature!r})'

    def __str__(self):
        return self.nomenclature

    def __int__(self):
        return self.span

    def __eq__(self, other):
        if isinstance(other, Freq):
            return other.nomenclature == self.nomenclature
        return str(other) == self.nomenclature

```

This works well for me and I'd think it may be implemented as an official feature to benefit others. I tried to make this as a PR but failed to find home for such python code in current codebase, the blocker is how to obtain `pybind11_builtins.pybind11_type` to serve as `AugmentCxxClass`' base class, or errors will occur. Though it can be obtained from any pybind defined wrapper class, even works crossing extension modules. I currently use a separate dummy extension module defining the dummy `PerfPack` class.

issue_title: Check unchecked types in certbot-dns-dnmadeeasy
issue_labels: [{'id': 277080619, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20code%20health', 'name': 'area: code health', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 263357278, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20testing', 'name': 'area: testing', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
This issue is for checking untyped definitions in the `certbot-dns-dnsmadeeasy` module.

To start, add the following to `mypy.ini` in the root of the repo:
```
[mypy-certbot_dns_dnsmadeeasy.*]
check_untyped_defs = True
```

Then run `tox -e mypy`. If passes, submit a PR. If not, fix errors and submit a PR when tests pass. You can read more about how all of this works below.

See explanation here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fp0tPP9bzYkUViTnOXAyoT_KfgTfs8HSpNMoU8Tm_SY/edit?usp=sharing

From http://blog.zulip.org/2016/10/13/static-types-in-python-oh-mypy/:

Add the `--check-untyped-defs` option to your mypy arguments, and get that running with no errors on the codebase. This option causes mypy to check every def in the codebase for internal consistency; mypy can detect many classes of bugs and mistakes in your codebase, without your having written a single type annotation! 

In most cases, you’ll want to fix bugs and bad code as you go, but you can also use #type: ignore annotations or exclude files to defer issues. For example, we excluded all of Zulip’s tests at first, since they’re both lower value to type-check and had most of the monkey-patching and other questionable Python in the project. For Zulip, getting clean `--check-untyped-defs` output took me about 2 days of hard work, including merging fixes for about 40 issues in the Zulip codebase. 

I spent another day or two generating nice reproducers for the mypy bugs I’d encountered and improving typeshed. Now that mypy is no longer in its infancy, mypy bugs are increasingly rare. But a large project should expect to encounter and fix typeshed issues (just submit a PR!). 

Once `mypy --check-untyped-defs` passes on your codebase, you should lock down your progress by running the mypy checker in your CI environment.

Because mypy type annotations are optional, once you’ve done the setup above, you can start annotating your codebase at whatever pace you like. Over time, you’ll get the benefits of static types in the parts of the codebase that you’ve annotated, without needing to do anything special to the rest of the codebase (the wonders of gradual typing!). In the next section, I’ll discuss strategy for how to move the codebase towards being fully annotated.

issue_title: Waiting for the completion of jobs.
issue_body:
Right now a SoS step waits for the completion of its job until they are complicated. This is the case for local and remote execution, and for rq and celery to some extend because they send all jobs within a step and wait for the completion of tasks at the end of step.

The wait process would block SoS in single processor mode, in interactive mode (which is essentially single). It occupies a "working" process during multi-process execution.

We should perhaps differentiate **running** jobs (jobs running as subprocess etc) and **waiting** jobs. In the former case, the main SoS process has to wait for the completion of the job and killing of SoS would kill the running jobs. In the latter case, the main SoS process could potentially exit, and check the results and resume execution when it resumes. Of course this requires complex status/signature matching to make sure SoS would not start another job or kill the running job when it resumes execution. Also, in the latter case, the **waiting** process should not occupy a dedicated process and do not block sos even in single processor mode. In another word, the **waiting** jobs would be assigned a dedicated status and then release its resource (process) once the jobs are submitted.

What all these requires would be some keyword like `wait=False` for remote job submission. In practice:

1. `wait=True` is the default value for remote execution where sos expect to get the result of execution when the command exits.

2. `wait=False`:
    * If there is a task status file. SoS knows that the job is still running and would return with *wait* status.
    * If there is a result status file (including error on failure). SoS knows the job is completed and return with **succ**.
    * If there is no task status file or result file. SoS runs the command and return with *wiat* status.

Not quite sure how this help us implement the cluster `qsub` mechanism but it certainly would help.

issue_title: SoS error message improvements
issue_body:
I've got feedback recently that the error messages we generate are not very easy to view (in fact they got daunted when seeing them and refused to read carefully ... ). There are 2 problems:

1. The trace-back information is long and distracting outside the context of [an interactive environment](https://github.com/vatlab/SoS/issues/867). 
2. Information from `docker` runs failed to point to where stderr files are kept.
3. Additionally for `task` the error info seems redundant and confusing. 

To elaborate, see MWE below for 1:

```
[sra (dump SRA to fastq): provides=['{data}_1.fastq', '{data}_2.fastq']]
output: f'{data}_1.fastq', f'{data}_2.fastq'
#task: workdir = '/tmp'
run: docker_image = 'gaow/debian-ngs', expand=True, stderr='/tmp/1.err', stdout='/tmp/1.out', workdir = '/tmp'
    fastq-dump -I --split-files {os.path.basename(data)}
```

and run:

```
sos run test.sos -t SRR001_1.fastq SRR002_1.fastq
```

Here is the output, red and daunting.

![2018-04-28-07-23-06_scrot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/917985/39396505-09d97d74-4ab5-11e8-9dca-a53b2d8d500e.png)

So for my point 1 above, I think here the `Traceback` info is completely unnecessary. We added it for #867. But is there a way to get rid of it here for runs from steps? The `RuntimeError: Executing script in docker returns an error (exitcode=3)` is more than enough.

For my point 2 above, I can improve it and report back here. I'll come back to my point 3 above when these two are fixed first.

issue_title: Document the default data proprocessing
issue_body: For example, torchvision MNIST is scaled to [0,1], whereas the standard MNIST dataset is in [0, 255]. This is not mentioned in the documentation, which instead just links to the original MNIST page, suggesting that the dataset is in the format described on that page. Similarly, standard CIFAR is in [0, 255] but torchvision CIFAR is in [-1, 1].

issue_title: Display a log rather than raise a FnapyPricingError when a list of EANs is provided
issue_body: In the previous versions of `fnapy`, only one EAN could be passed to `query_pricing` and it made sense to raise an exception when something was wrong. Now, `query_pricing` accepts a list of EANs and raising the exception would prevent the user from having the results even if only one EAN is not found.

issue_title: Allow capture of imported modules to become a `require` at runtime
issue_body:
**Note**: The originally reported issue below was addressed with https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi-aws/pull/262.  This issue is now tracking the proposals in https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi/issues/1588#issuecomment-401968174

In https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi-aws/issues/249, @rgwood reported that we had missed the `qs` and `uuid` modules imported within a function.  And indeed, we do.  This necessitates manually listing the packages to get them included in the function.  The code looks exactly as I think it should be written -- and how we want it to be written -- but I'm guessing something to do with the function code being separated into its own file tricked our module import capture detection logic.

issue_title: Ray fails to serialize self-reference objects
issue_body:
<!--
General questions should be asked on the mailing list ray-dev@googlegroups.com.

Before submitting an issue, please fill out the following form.
-->

### System information
- **Ray installed from (source or binary)**: pip
- **Ray version**: 0.2.2
- **Python version**: 3.6.2

<!--
You can obtain the Ray version with

python -c "import ray; print(ray.__version__)"
-->

### Describe the problem

Ray fails to serialize self-reference objects (for example, Graph objects in networkx).

I think it is because ray always tries to use pyarrow first and does not catch `pyarrow.lib.ArrowNotImplementedError`, see

https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/e0360eb4298371e308cd266dd512befaa457ce24/python/ray/worker.py#L285-L289

After catching `pyarrow.lib.ArrowNotImplementedError`, we **should not** use `use_dict=True` as a workaround, because it will cause endless loop. A correct approach may be:

```python
            except (pyarrow.SerializationCallbackError, pyarrow.lib.ArrowNotImplementedError) as e:
                try:
                    if isinstance(e, pyarrow.lib.ArrowNotImplementedError):
                        e.example_object = value
                        raise e  # redirect to use cloudpickle
```

### Source code / logs
<!-- Include any logs or source code that would be helpful to diagnose the problem. If including tracebacks, please include the full traceback. Large logs and files should be attached. Try to provide a reproducible test case that is the bare minimum necessary to generate the problem. -->

```python
class Graph:
    def __init__(self):
        self.g = self

G = Graph()
ray.put(G)  # --> pyarrow.lib.ArrowNotImplementedError: This object exceeds the maximum recursion depth. It may contain itself recursively.

# another example

import networkx as nx
G = nx.Graph()
    
G.add_edges_from([(1, 2), (1, 3)])
G.add_node(1)
G.add_edge(1, 2)
G.add_node("spam")  # adds node "spam"
G.add_nodes_from("spam")  # adds 4 nodes: 's', 'p', 'a', 'm'
G.add_edge(3, 'm')
ray.put(G)  # --> pyarrow.lib.ArrowNotImplementedError: This object exceeds the maximum recursion depth. It may contain itself recursively.
```

@mitar

issue_title: Casting a LocalizedValue to string seems slow
issue_body:
