issue_title: Dataset add `--auto-match` option is redundant
issue_body:
- It's enabled by default even if not supplied
- It suggests that without it there is a manual product association, but no such thing exists
- Manual product association is not even possible due to handling of lineage datasets

`dataset add` always performs matching of dataset definition document to a set of product definitions. A set of products being matched against is either

- Full set of products in the database (default behaviour)
- Subset of products from the database (product to match against are specified with multiple `--dtype` options)
- Subset of products from the database with some modifications loaded from a file with `--match-rules` option (I suggest we get rid of this one)

Because `dataset add` supports adding datasets found in the lineage subdocument to the database there is no way to supply "manual" `product<->dataset` relation in a general case.

issue_title: rework Instrument API to allow efficient implementation
issue_body:
The Instrument API potentially adds overhead, since we have to call the hooks at all kinds of low level places that might be happening in tight loops. I'm not really worried about the overhead right now, but we should make sure that if we *do* decide to optimize it in the future, that we won't have to break the API to do so.

I think there are two parts to this:

* Instead of blindly letting users muck around with the list of instruments, provide some explicit API for mutations. This is crucial because it lets us preprocess the list.

* Make sure that we can tell by examining an Instrument object which hooks it actually provides. This way our preprocessing can tell things like "there are no Instruments registered for this event, don't even bother looking".

issue_title: "Press enter to terminate" message is confusing
issue_body:
Performing a Thread DFU will print a `Press <ENTER> to terminate` message...

https://github.com/NordicSemiconductor/pc-nrfutil/blob/202fc2850deadfb8dbf40460f53f75033673b8cb/nordicsemi/__main__.py#L913

...while the NCP is trying to be promoted to a router, but from a naïve user point of view, what I got is:

```
C:\Users\....\pc-nrfutil>nrfutil dfu thread -pkg ...\app_dfu_package.zip -p COM10
Address not specified. Using ff03::1 (all Thread nodes)
Using connectivity board at serial port: COM10
Press <ENTER> to terminate
```

i.e. as a user, my first reaction is to think "this has finished successfully without error, and is asking me to press enter to end". This is not the case.

If a thread DFU procedure is going to print messages in non-verbose mode, please make those messages be more informative. e.g. `Attempting to promote NCP to router status, press <ENTER> to cancel`.

Also:
- Please use `Ctrl`+`C` as the key combination to press when wanting to cancel stuff
- Please use `enter` as the key to press when confirming something or accept a default action

issue_title: Make utf-8-sig the default encoding for reading
issue_body:
Since Excel will write CSV in UTF-8 with BOM, it will be a common requirement to be able to read this. So making `utf-8-sig` the default UTF-8 encoding will make a bigger crowd happy at a very small risk of making some unhappy (but having CSV column names start with `0xffef` will make them unhappy no matter what).

Probably the best place to implement this change is in `dsv.normalize_encoding`.

issue_title: 为 visualMap 新增 pieces 参数
issue_body:
为 visualMap 组件新增了 pieces 参数，该参数仅在 is_piecewise 为 True 时生效。适用于所有支持 visualMap 组件的图形种类，

```
:param pieces:
        自定义『分段式视觉映射组件（visualMapPiecewise）』的每一段的范围，
        以及每一段的文字，以及每一段的特别的样式。例如：
        pieces: [
            {min: 1500}, // 不指定 max，表示 max 为无限大（Infinity）。
            {min: 900, max: 1500},
            {min: 310, max: 1000},
            {min: 200, max: 300},
            {min: 10, max: 200, label: '10 到 200（自定义label）'},
            // 表示 value 等于 123 的情况。
            {value: 123, label: '123（自定义特殊颜色）', color: 'grey'},
            {max: 5}     // 不指定 min，表示 min 为无限大（-Infinity）。
        ]
```
## Map

代码
```python
def test_visualmap_pieces():
    value = [155, 10, 66, 78]
    attr = ["福建", "山东", "北京", "上海"]
    map = Map("全国地图示例", width=1200, height=600)
    map.add("", attr, value, maptype='china',
            is_visualmap=True, is_piecewise=True,
            visual_text_color="#000",
            visual_range_text=["", ""],
            pieces=[
                {
                    "max": 160,
                    "min": 70,
                    "label": "高数值"
                },
                {
                    "max": 69,
                    "min": 0,
                    "label": "低数值"
                },
            ])
    map.render()
```

效果图

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19553554/36943053-22748c70-1fbd-11e8-86dc-ac3d48f5c4e3.png)

可以看到，VisualMap 的标签已经被自定义了

## Geo

代码
```python
def test_geo_visualmap_pieces():
    data = [
        ("海门", 9), ("鄂尔多斯", 12), ("招远", 12),
        ("舟山", 12), ("齐齐哈尔", 14), ("盐城", 15)
    ]
    geo = Geo("全国主要城市空气质量", "data from pm2.5", **style.init_style)
    attr, value = geo.cast(data)
    geo.add("", attr, value, type="effectScatter", is_random=True,
            is_visualmap=True, is_piecewise=True,
            visual_text_color="#fff",
            pieces=[
                {"min": 0, "max": 13, "label": "0 < x < 13"},
                {"min": 14, "max": 16, "label": "14 < x < 16"},
            ],
            effect_scale=5)
    geo.render()
```

效果图

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19553554/36943214-f0133fd0-1fbf-11e8-97c6-573a8fb25395.png)

issue_title: Impossible to save SPECAIR spectrum in radis' database
issue_body:
```
from radis.tools.database import SpecDatabase
from pyspecair import one_slab
s = one_slab(wavelength_min=473.9,         # nm
                     wavelength_max=526.1,    # nm
                     Tgas=30000, 
                     pressure=1,            # bar
                     mole_fractions={'N II':0.001, 
                                     'e-':0.1,
                                     },
                     self_absorption=False,
                     verbose=False)
db = SpecDatabase(r'../database') #where is my folder "database"
db.add(s)
```
The above code does not work, due to the following bug:
```
  File "<ipython-input-102-f89865316461>", line 1, in <module>
    runfile('/home/minou/Python/Article_NII/FID1/fit2.py', wdir='/home/minou/Python/Article_NII/FID1')

  File "/home/minou/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/spyder/utils/site/sitecustomize.py", line 705, in runfile
    execfile(filename, namespace)

  File "/home/minou/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/spyder/utils/site/sitecustomize.py", line 102, in execfile
    exec(compile(f.read(), filename, 'exec'), namespace)

  File "/home/minou/Python/Article_NII/FID1/fit2.py", line 27, in <module>
    db.add(s)

  File "/home/minou/Python/radis/radis/tools/database.py", line 828, in add
    raise ValueError('Unvalid Spectrum type: {0}'.format(type(spectrum)))
```
It seems that the type of spectrum generated by pyspecar via one_slab is not correct for database.

issue_title: simplejson 2.3.1: simplejson.tests.test_namedtuple.TestNamedTuple.test_namedtuple_dumps() fails when simplejson/_speedups.so extension module is present
issue_body:
If _speedups.so extension has been built, then test_namedtuple_dumps fails and a segmentation fault  can also occur. This regression has been introduced in simplejson 2.3.1. 

```
$ python2.7 setup.py build_ext -i
...
$ python2.7 simplejson/tests/__init__.py
..........................................................................F...................................................................................
======================================================================
FAIL: test_namedtuple_dumps (simplejson.tests.test_namedtuple.TestNamedTuple)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/simplejson-2.3.1/simplejson/tests/test_namedtuple.py", line 54, in test_namedtuple_dumps
    self.assertEqual(d, json.loads(json.dumps(v)))
AssertionError: OrderedDict([('value', 1)]) != [1]

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 158 tests in 2.785s

FAILED (failures=1)
$ python2.7 simplejson/tests/__init__.py
..........................................................................F........................................................................Segmentation fault
```

Deletion of *.pyc files avoids segmentation fault.
